Oracc: SAAo/SAA01 — The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West

SAA 01 206. Sending Borers to the King (ABL 0155)[via saao/saa01]

Obverse
o 1o 1

a-na LUGAL be-lí-ía

(1) To the king, my lord: your servant Zeru-ibni. Good health to the king, my lord!

o 22

ARAD-ka mNUMUN—ib-ni

o 33

lu DI-mu a-na LUGAL be-lí-ía

o 44

šu-uḫ LÚv.GAR.U.U-MEŠ

(4) As to the (stone) borers about whom the king, my lord, wrote to me, I have just sent five borers to the king, my lord.

o 55

ša LUGAL be-lí

o 66

iš-pur-an-ni

o 77

an-nu-rig 05 LÚv.GAR.U.U-MEŠ

o 88

[a]-⸢na⸣ LUGAL be-lí-ía

o 99

⸢ú*-se-bi*-la⸣

rest broken away

(Rest destroyed)

Reverse
rr uninscribed

Adapted from Simo Parpola, The Correspondence of Sargon II, Part I: Letters from Assyria and the West (State Archives of Assyria, 1), 1987. Lemmatised by Mikko Luukko, 2009-11, as part of the AHRC-funded research project “Mechanisms of Communication in an Ancient Empire: The Correspondence between the King of Assyria and his Magnates in the 8th Century BC” (AH/F016581/1; University College London) directed by Karen Radner. The annotated edition is released under the Creative Commons Attribution Share-Alike license 3.0. Please cite this page as http://oracc.org/saao/P334101/.
